introduction: An acute infectious disease of humans (particularly children) caused by any of three serotypes of poliovirus P1, P2, and P3. Immunity to one serotype does not confer immunity to others. Occurs in many regions of the developing world. There was an increase in global cases as a result of an epidemic in India. In 2015, Nigeria was declared a polio free country.

features: Incubation period: 6 - 20 days, with a range of 3 – 35 days
Irritability and pain or stiffness of the back, arms, legs, abdomen
Muscle tenderness and spasms in any part of the body
Headache, stiff neck and back, asymmetric muscle weakness
Location of paralysis depends on region affected

investigations: Viral isolation from stool, pharynx or cerebrospinal fluid
Fingerprinting genomic sequencing to determine if it is the wild type vaccine type
Serology: a fourfold rise in antibody may be demonstrated
Cerebrospinal fluid examination: Raised white cell count, 10 - 200 cells/mm3 (primarily lymphocytes), Mild increase in protein: 40 - 50 mg/mL

goals: Prepare the child and family for a prolonged management of permanent disability if it seems likely
non_drug: Avoidance of exertion and application of hot packs
Lying on a firm bed and hospitalization for those with paralytic disease
Suitable body alignment to avoid excessive skeletal deformity
Active and passive motions as soon as pain disappears
Tracheostomy in case of vocal cord paralysis
drug: Bethanecol 5 - 10 mg orally or 2.5 - 5 mg subcutaneously for bladder paralysis
Avoid opiates if there is impairment of ventilation
Treat urinary tract infection with appropriate antibiotics
adverse_reactions_and_cautions: Oral polio vaccine should not be administered to persons who are immunocompromised (it is a live vaccine)
Contraindicated in persons with history of severe allergic reaction to a vaccine component or following prior dose
Inactivated vaccine may be used in immune compromised persons

complications: Muscles of respiration and swallowing which can be fatal
prevention: To prevent / limit contamination of food and water by the virus
Vaccination: The only effective method of Prevention
Oral Polio Vaccine Given at: Birth, 6 weeks,10 weeks,14 weeks
Limits spread of wild polio virus
Inactivated Polio Vaccine Given at 2 months 4 months 12 months
Duration of immunity not known with certainty
